mentions.note: Largest credit card issuer by balances post-Discover acquisition with greater scale, diversification, and operating leverage vs. Bread; owned by travel trust for durability; cleaner way to play consumer lending thesis.
Down 1.66%; credit indicator showing stress; now 80 points from high, down significantly; rising rates and consumer stress signal.
Absolute favorite bank stock; buyback-funded, Discover merger on track, cap-gains tax cap threat fading. Optimistic 12–24 month outlook.
Has almost no exposure to private credit issues; trading at 9x earnings; even though chart is horrible, good level to start if don't own any; continue to own position.
Better credit card play vs. Synchrony; pulled back nicely to $225 from $259; Cramer prefers for travel-trust exposure.

Mixed quarter (revenue beat, EPS miss) and $5B Brex acquisition caused 16% decline. Executing on full-service financial services strategy; valuation at 11x earnings offers opportunity if closes gap to 16x.
Owns plenty of cash and significant buyback; credit card rate cap risk passed; key to watch: Discover card network strategy; report Thursday.
Recommended previously by Cramer; holding at current levels; caller asked about regional bank positioning.
Bank benefiting from deregulation narrative and equity-issuance/M&A activity acceleration to offset long-standing valuation discount.
Cheapest bank at 12x earnings post-Discover acquisition; rate cut cycle assured given Trump Fed replacement candidates; buying back stock.
Same charge-down as American Express; credit card sector experiencing similar compression; not a concern.
